key_insight: Geometry-based leverage score sampling can pick a much smaller but similarly informative subset of ASSYST structures for training linear ACE potentials.
engineering_relevance: This reduces DFT labeling cost by about 2–3× while preserving energy, force, and defect-level accuracy, making MLIP development faster and cheaper.
method: Build linear ACE potentials on ASSYST-generated structure pools.
Compare leverage-guided CUR-type subset selection with random, energy-based, and force-based sampling.
Validate on Al and test transfer on Cu and Al-Cu alloys.
evidence_signal: Leverage-guided subsets reached plateau-level energy and force accuracy using roughly 30–40% of labeled configurations, with comparable defect energetics once chemical diversity was sufficient.
watch_item: Performance depends on having a representative structure pool and enough chemical diversity, so overly narrow pools may limit transferability.
paper_title: Data-Efficient Training of Linear ACE Potentials through Leverage-Guided Subset Selection of ASSYST Structure Pools
